name: Thrones
description: In Abrahamic cosmology, particularly within Christian angelology and Jewish mysticism, the Thrones (also known as Ophanim or Galgalim) represent a high order of angels. The Thrones are often associated with divine justice, authority, and the very presence of God. Scholarly interpretations suggest they are celestial beings who embody God's judgment and power, acting as carriers or chariots for the divine throne itself. They are often depicted as wheels or thrones covered in eyes, reflecting their constant vigilance and unwavering devotion to God's will. The concept of Thrones evolved over time, drawing from earlier Mesopotamian and Near Eastern traditions of celestial hierarchies. Their role is to maintain cosmic order and execute divine decrees, making them integral to the structure of the heavens. These angels are not merely passive observers but active participants in upholding the divine plan, reflecting the multifaceted nature of angelic beings in Abrahamic religions. Their appearance and function highlight the awe-inspiring and often incomprehensible nature of the divine realm.
subtitle: Embodiments of Divine Justice

keyMyths.title: Vision of Ezekiel's Wheel
keyMyths.description: The prophet Ezekiel's vision is central to understanding the Thrones. Ezekiel describes seeing wheels within wheels, covered in eyes, accompanying the divine chariot. This vision is interpreted as representing the Thrones, celestial beings embodying God's power and movement throughout the cosmos. The wheels' intricate structure and omnipresent eyes symbolize their knowledge and vigilance, reflecting the divine oversight of creation. This passage in Ezekiel is crucial for visual depictions and theological understandings of the Thrones.

keyMyths.title: Thrones as Carriers of God's Throne
keyMyths.description: Some interpretations posit that the Thrones literally carry or embody God's throne. This highlights their role as direct servants of the divine, constantly supporting and upholding God's authority. Their unyielding devotion and unwavering obedience make them the perfect vessels for the divine presence. This concept emphasizes the hierarchical structure of the celestial realm, with the Thrones playing a vital role in maintaining the stability of the divine order. Their existence is intrinsically linked to God's reign and power.

keyMyths.title: Thrones and Divine Judgement
keyMyths.description: The association of Thrones with divine judgment is significant. They are believed to be involved in the execution of God's justice, ensuring that divine decrees are carried out. Their role in this process reflects the importance of justice and order in the divine plan. The Thrones serve as a reminder that God's judgment is ever-present and that actions have consequences. They are not merely instruments of punishment but also upholders of righteousness and fairness.
